evidence_quotes.quote: video games remain playable even after official server shutdown or end of support.

paragraphs.text: I appreciate the Digital Fairness Acts goal of enhancing consumer protection in digital markets. I wish to express my support for its alignment with the objectives of the Stop Destroying Videogames initiative (often called StopKillingGames). This European Citizens Initiative urges legislation that ensures video games remain playable even after official server shutdown or end of support. From my experience, games sold as permanent products often rely on servers that publishers can switch offsometimes permanently disabling the game. This effectively erases products consumers have already purchased. Stop Destroying Videogames demands practical solutions such as offline modes, community-server tools, or end-of‐life planning that guarantee ongoing usability without undue burden on developers These goals complement the Digital Fairness Acts intent to protect consumers from unfair terms and exploitative practices. So I respectfully request that the Act explicitly include provisions addressing the destruction or disabling of digital-only productsespecially video gameswith no expiry date at the time of purchase. Ensuring continued access after support ends is essential to uphold consumer rights, maintain trust, and preserve cultural heritage in digital form. Thank you for considering this perspective. I fully support the Act in principle and hope it embraces robust protections aligned with StopDestroyingVideogames, safeguarding both consumers and the creative longevity of digital art.
